rationale: Both subtests exercise real code, not constants. (a) writes a real config.yaml, calls Load then PanelGateReviewerSlots("bead") — which resolves the top-level panel.reviewers via resolveGateReviewers and runs expandSlots -> r.model(). slots[0].Model=="codex" is produced ONLY by the empty-string->Family fallback in model() (config.go:150); break that fallback and model() returns "", so the assertion fails. Genuinely end-to-end. (b) writes {model:""} with no family; Load fails via validateReviewerEntries's neither-set branch (config.go:560), which runs (line 522) BEFORE the sum-floor (538); count:1 avoids the count<1 branch, sum==2 avoids the floor, YAML is valid — so for this input the ONLY error naming "panel.reviewers[0]"+"recovery:" is the neither-set branch. Right reason, not spurious. Verified: go test -run passes both, full package green, build clean, fence=2 files, git status clean, model()/validateReviewerEntries comments accurately describe behavior.
findings: Subtest (a) is a genuine end-to-end resolve-to-family proof: slots[0].Model=="codex" is only reachable through the real Load -> resolveGateReviewers (top-level fallback) -> expandSlots -> r.model() empty-string->Family path. Removing the fallback fails the test.

Subtest (b) fails Load for the RIGHT reason: validateReviewerEntries neither-set branch runs before the sum-floor; count:1 and sum==2 rule out the count<1 and sum<2 messages, and the YAML is valid, so "panel.reviewers[0]"+"recovery:" uniquely pins the neither-set branch for this input.

Minor (non-blocking): the (b) assertion checks "recovery:" (generic across messages) rather than the distinctive "sets neither" phrase; combined with "panel.reviewers[0]" it still uniquely pins the branch for this input, so the pin holds, but asserting "sets neither" would be strictly tighter against future index-0 refactors.
sum>=2 floor is out of scope for this reconciliation and already covered by existing 112 tests (unchanged); no missing case for this bead.
Empirical: go test ./internal/config -run TestLoad_EmptyStringModel -v = both PASS; full package green; go build ./... clean; git show --name-only = config.go + config_test.go only; git status clean.
